original_abstract: Global labor markets are currently undergoing profound transformations driven by socio-political instability, rising structural inequality, worsening employment insecurity, and deteriorating working conditions. In response to these intensifying challenges, international institutions have issued various policy frameworks designed to safeguard and promote the health and well-being of the global workforce. This article examines the strategic integration of the International Labour Organization’s Decent Work Agenda within the broader policy frameworks of the World Health Organization and the World Bank, illustrating how these collaborative agendas shape contemporary labor standards.
humanized_abstract: The World of Work in the 21st century is experiencing significant changes driven by a multitude of socio-political factors, including political instability, increasing structural inequality, job and working conditions insecurity and poor work environment and the list of challenges is growing by the day. To counter this growing number of challenges, a series of policy instruments have been developed at the international level with the objective of protecting and promoting workers health and well-being. This paper examines the strategic positioning of the ILO Decent Work Agenda within the health policies of the WHO and the WB, hence, showing the ways in which labour standards are being defined through the implementation of such collaborative policies.
